entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Cultivated species Arachis hypogaea L. used for genetic improvement

entities.name: WILD RELATIVES
entities.description: Diploid wild species of section Arachis used to introduce new genes

entities.name: SYNTHETIC AMPHIDIPLOIDS
entities.description: Plants created from combinations of diploid wild species to serve as genetic bridges

entities.name: A AND B GENOMES
entities.description: The two types of genomes represented in the wild species of section Arachis

entities.name: SECTION ARACHIS
entities.description: The taxonomic section containing both wild and cultivated Arachis species

entities.name: BIVALENT AND MULTIVALENT ASSOCIATIONS
entities.description: Chromosome pairing patterns used to confirm genome identity in amphidiploids

entities.name: ARACHIS BATIZOCOI
entities.description: A wild species possessing a distinct B genome partially homologous to the A genome

entities.name: CROSSABILITY
entities.description: The capacity for hybridization between A. hypogaea and synthetic amphidiploids

entities.name: CHROMOSOME PAIRING
entities.description: The association of homologous chromosomes in hybrids

entities.name: POLLEN AND POD FERTILITY
entities.description: The reproductive viability of hybrids between A. hypogaea and amphidiploids

entities.name: HYBRIDS
entities.description: Offspring produced from the crossing of A. hypogaea and synthetic amphidiploids

entities.name: GENETIC BRIDGE
entities.description: The mechanism by which amphidiploids facilitate the transfer of genes from wild species to cultivated crops

entities.name: GENE TRANSFER
entities.description: The process of moving genetic material from wild relatives into the cultivated groundnut
